body: WARRANT#94 shipped, compiled clean, synced. Ran the systematic brainstorm you asked for: 10 metaphor-derived candidates (EEG coherence, fMRI co-activation, LIDAR triangulation, sonar matched filtering, radio interferometry, seismic lag triangulation, multi-modal medical fusion, weather-ensemble parameter sensitivity, GPS multilateration, Doppler) evaluated in parallel against the actual code -- which ones already exist, which are grounded, which are too speculative. Converged on ONE real winner: weather-ensemble/parameter-sensitivity, aka plateau detection. It directly targets the exact overfit pattern behind every STAND DOWN result you saw: P3B picks the single best of 562,500 weight combos, and a lucky spike looks identical to a real edge from one point estimate. New check perturbs the found weights +/-15% and re-scores on the same sample -- if nearby weights collapse in performance (std>=15pp), that's a narrow spike, and it now triggers the SAME regularization pull the fit-check already uses. Only makes acceptance stricter, never looser. The other 9 candidates: 3 were already built into the codebase (sonar/matched-filtering=ProbDB, multi-modal fusion=the existing weight blend, GPS-RAIM=basically what caught last pass's mislabeling bug), 3 stay parked as real-but-speculative for now (fMRI co-activation, seismic lag, Doppler) rather than chased without evidence. Ledger at pass_76, source at WARRANT#94. Still not live-tested -- terminal wasn't running this whole pass. Next attach is the real test of #91 through #94 together.
